pattern: In a DAG of 'stronger than' edges, a team nobody beats has no incoming edge -- the champion is unambiguous only when exactly one node has in-degree zero.
hints: A team can only be the unbeaten champion if no edge points into it; start by finding every team with no incoming edge.
Tally how many edges point at each team (in-degree); the candidates for champion are exactly the teams with in-degree zero.
If there is exactly one team with in-degree zero, that is the champion; if there are zero or more than one, no single team can be declared the champion, so return -1.

edge_inputs.description: single team, no edges at all
simple chain of three, champion at the top
two teams tie for having no one beat them -- no unique champion
no edges at all among several teams -- everyone is unbeaten, so no unique champion
a diamond DAG where two intermediate teams both lose to the same single top team
five teams, total order given out of index order

stress.description: a random total ranking is drawn, then a random subset of consistent edges is kept
